builtFor.tagline: Any team that works by shifts and schedules
builtFor.pitchBody: Proven playbooks for sales teams, arbitrage teams, restaurants, retail, services, production, and IT – pick your industry.

hero.title: {label}: a schedule without chaos and shifts covered on time
hero.subtitle: Stop juggling spreadsheets and chasing people in chats. See your whole team's month, catch coverage gaps before they turn into an empty dining room.

1.pain: A waiter no-shows on a busy Friday - you find out an hour before service.
1.fix: You see the coverage gap days in advance: an alert fires the moment a shift gets thin.

2.pain: Every schedule change means phone calls, three chat threads and re-sending the spreadsheet.
2.fix: Change a shift in one click. The staffer gets a notification, the team sees the live version.

3.pain: Weekends swing between too many shifts and overpaying, or short staff and chaos in the kitchen.
3.fix: Smengo shows coverage by role: «Kitchen · Sat - 2 slots open». You fix it ahead of time.

features.title: Built for shifts in the kitchen and the floor
features.subtitle: Six tools that cover 90% of what a restaurant manager does every week.

schedule.title: A month at a glance
schedule.desc: Every kitchen, bar and floor shift on a single screen. Rows are people, columns are days, no separate sheets.

telegram.title: Telegram bot for the team
telegram.desc: Staff see their shifts in Telegram. One-tap check-in, late arrivals highlighted automatically.

swaps.title: Shift swaps without chats
swaps.desc: A barista can't make it - a replacement claims it from the feed in a minute. Manager approves in one click.

ai.title: AI scheduling assistant
ai.desc: Smengo proposes next week's schedule for you, factoring in hours, time off and typical shift load.

hr.title: HR dashboard
hr.desc: See who's overworked, whose review is coming up, who hasn't finished paperwork - before it becomes a problem.

onboarding.title: Onboarding for new hires

roi.title: Don't overpay for extra shifts. Don't lose revenue when you're short-staffed.
roi.subtitle: Smengo shows coverage by role and by hour - you can see where a shift is thin, and where you've put two people in a spot that only needs one.

items.1: Average shift coverage as a clear metric

items.3: Alerts when someone is on track to exceed hours this month

features.title:
Everything you need.
Nothing you don't.
features.subtitle: Smengo shows the whole month at once: employees as rows, days as columns.
features.f1Desc: One screen shows your entire team's schedule for the full month. No scrolling, no pivot tables.
features.f2Desc: Group employees by team. Filter instantly to focus on just the department you need right now.
features.f3Desc: Set a minimum headcount per department. Smengo flags any day that falls short - quietly, not alarmingly.
features.f4Desc: Define your own shift types with labels, colors, and a "counts as present" toggle - in any language.
features.f5Desc: Full support for Russian, Ukrainian, and English. Set a company default; each user can override their own.
features.f6Desc: Managers see their team. Owners see everything. No accidental edits from the wrong person.

faq.title: Common questions.
faq.q1: Do I need a credit card to start?
faq.a1: No. The 14-day trial requires no payment information whatsoever. You only enter billing details if you decide to upgrade after the trial ends.
faq.q2: Can I import my team from Excel?
faq.a2: Yes - import employees via CSV. Direct Excel integration is on the roadmap for later this year.
faq.a3: The full interface is available in Russian, Ukrainian, and English. Set a company-wide default, and each user can override their own preference from their profile.
faq.a4: Plans are billed monthly or annually (annual saves 20%). Upgrade, downgrade, or cancel any time from the Billing page. All payments are processed securely via Paddle.
faq.a5: Absolutely. Upgrade or downgrade any time - changes take effect immediately, and any unused time is prorated.

whyus.title:
Built for managers
who hate spreadsheets.
whyus.sub: Every feature is here because a real ops manager asked for it.
whyus.i1Desc: Every employee, every day - no tabs, no scrolling. Full picture in seconds.
whyus.i2Desc: Auto-flag days when a department falls below minimum headcount - before the shift starts.
whyus.i3Desc: Click a cell to assign a shift. Drag across a range to fill a whole week instantly.
whyus.i4Desc: Owners see everything. Managers see only their teams. No accidental edits by the wrong person.
whyus.i5Desc: Name your shifts, pick colors, mark what counts as present. Works in any language.
whyus.i6Desc: One click to export - ready to print, share in chat, or attach to an email.
whyus.i7Desc: Fill and analyze schedules with an AI assistant that learns your team's patterns.
whyus.i8Desc: Staff mark themselves present via Telegram bot or the web app - no phone calls needed.
whyus.i9Desc: Full access from day one. Enter billing details only if you choose to upgrade.
whyus.i10Desc: Connect your tools, scripts, or bots. Build on top of Smengo with a documented REST API.

hero.title: Any employee — in two seconds.

bento.title: One card instead of five spreadsheets

bento.t5Desc: Who changed what is always visible. No more "who edited this?" debates.
bento.t6Desc: The card and the shifts are one loop: statuses, vacations and hours stay in sync.

seo.ogDescription: Build staff schedules, see coverage, track hours, manage swaps, and keep every change in one system.

seo.appDescription: Online shift schedule planner with a grid view, coverage alerts, hour tracking, and Telegram notifications.

hero.title: Your whole team’s schedule on one screen. No spreadsheets, no chaos.

ways.aiReassurance: You can always edit by hand

vs.title: Excel vs Smengo
vs.subtitle: A spreadsheet is fine for a draft. But when shifts move, people swap, and coverage needs to be visible ahead of time, the schedule has to work like an operating system.
vs.excelNote: works until the schedule starts changing every day
vs.smengoNote: one workflow for schedules, coverage, and changes
vs.r1Excel: The file gets copied into chat and the latest version disappears
vs.r1Smengo: Everyone sees one live schedule grid
vs.r2Excel: Coverage gaps surface on the day of the shift
vs.r2Smengo: Alerts show coverage risk ahead of time
vs.r3Excel: Hours, overtime, and days off are counted by hand
vs.r3Smengo: Hours and statuses are calculated automatically
vs.r4Excel: Swaps and edits are scattered across messages
vs.r4Smengo: Every change lands in one place
vs.r5Excel: Hard to see who changed what
vs.r5Smengo: Edit history and access roles stay under control
vs.pill1: One version for managers and staff
vs.pill2: Coverage visible before the shift starts
vs.footerText: Smengo does not just draw a schedule — it runs the workflow around every shift.

rest.c1Note: Fewer chat questions and fewer manual confirmations.

proof.title: What changes for teams
proof.subtitle: Smengo turns shift scheduling from a manual spreadsheet into a managed workflow: fewer questions, fewer mistakes, and a faster start to the week.
proof.quote: Building the schedule used to take a whole day. Now we put it together in 30 minutes and nobody is confused about who works when.
proof.author: Typical rollout scenario
proof.stub: no Excel files, no late-night edits
proof.imageAlt: Manager looking at a shift schedule on a tablet
proof.imageCaption: A restaurant, retail, or service team scenario: the manager sees the month, coverage, and changes in one place.
proof.caseTitle: A 24-person team moved from a shared spreadsheet to a live schedule
proof.caseText: Instead of forwarding an Excel file, the manager runs the schedule in Smengo: staff see current shifts, and disputed edits stay in history.
proof.caseB1: fewer questions about which version is current
proof.caseB2: coverage gaps and unfilled shifts are visible before the week starts
proof.caseB3: swaps and manual edits do not disappear in chat
proof.flow1Title: Spreadsheets, Excel, and vibe-coded custom tools
proof.flow1Text: The schedule lives in files, formulas, copies, and scripts that usually depend on one person.
proof.flow2Text: It is unclear which shift is current, who changed it, and why coverage dropped.
proof.flow3Title: Rules become part of the workflow
proof.flow3Text: Roles, access, vacations, swaps, and limits move from chats into one managed process.
proof.flow4Title: A live schedule instead of a file
proof.flow4Text: Employees see current shifts, while managers see risks early and keep a change history.
proof.signal1: one current schedule version for the whole team
proof.signal2: coverage, gaps, and overload are visible before the week starts
proof.signal3: manual edits, swaps, and decisions do not disappear in chats
proof.resultLine: Result: fewer questions, fewer mistakes, and a faster recurring schedule launch.

final.title: Build your first schedule in 30 minutes
final.subtitle: Free 14-day trial. No card. Cancel anytime.

pricing.title: One schedule costs less than one missed shift
pricing.subtitle: 14 days free on any plan. No credit card.
pricing.ctaSubtitle: 14 days free on any plan. No card needed.
pricing.faq1q: Do I need a credit card for the trial?
pricing.faq1a: No. 14 days of full access without a card. After that, choose a plan or stay on limited read-only access.
pricing.faq2q: Can I change my plan later?
pricing.faq2a: Yes, anytime from the Billing section. Upgrades are prorated; downgrades take effect next cycle.
pricing.faq3q: What happens to my data if I cancel?
pricing.faq3a: Your data is kept. You switch to read-only mode - you can export schedules at any time.
pricing.faq4a: For teams over 300 people, contact us and we'll work out custom terms.
pricing.planStartDesc: For small teams just getting started
pricing.planTeamDesc: For growing teams that need coverage and alerts
pricing.planBusinessDesc: For larger operations with multiple departments and roles
pricing.socialProof: Trusted by teams in restaurants, clinics, retail and IT
pricing.socialProofStat: We're launching: early access for the first teams
pricing.enterpriseDesc: For companies with 300+ employees – custom terms, SSO and a dedicated manager

terms.metaDescription: The terms that govern your use of Smengo, the team shift scheduling service.
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service (the “Terms”) govern access to and use of Smengo — a web application for planning team work schedules, available at smengo.com (the “Service”). The Service is operated by the Smengo team (“Smengo”, “we”). By registering an account or using the Service, you confirm that you have read, understood and agree to be bound by these Terms and our Privacy Policy. If you use the Service on behalf of a company, you confirm that you are authorised to accept these Terms for that company.
terms.serviceBody: Smengo lets organisations plan and track team work schedules: a monthly grid of employees and days, work statuses (working day, day off, vacation, sick leave and custom statuses), departments, coverage alerts, exports and multi-user access with roles. The feature set depends on the selected plan and may evolve over time.
terms.accountsBody: To use the Service you must create an account with accurate, up-to-date information and keep it accurate. You are responsible for safeguarding your credentials and for all activity under your account. You must be at least 18 years old and have the legal capacity to enter into this agreement. Organisation owners and administrators control who has access to their organisation’s data and are responsible for the actions of users they invite.
terms.billingBody:
New organisations receive a free 14-day trial with no payment card required. When the trial ends, continued full use of the Service requires a paid subscription; otherwise the account switches to a limited read-only mode and your data is preserved.
Payments are processed by Paddle (Paddle.com Market Ltd / Paddle.com Inc.), our Merchant of Record. Paddle is the seller of record: it handles checkout, invoicing, payment methods, sales taxes / VAT and refunds in accordance with Paddle’s own terms (paddle.com/legal). By purchasing a subscription you also agree to Paddle’s Buyer Terms.
Subscriptions renew automatically for the chosen billing period until cancelled. You can cancel at any time, with effect from the end of the current paid period. Prices may change; we will notify you of price increases in advance, and they take effect from the next billing period. Refunds are handled in accordance with Paddle’s refund policy unless the law of your country requires otherwise.
terms.customerDataBody: All data your organisation adds to the Service — employee names, departments, schedules, statuses and other records (“Customer Data”) — belongs to your organisation. We process Customer Data only to provide the Service, as described in our Privacy Policy. You are responsible for ensuring that you have a lawful basis to add personal data of your employees to the Service and for the accuracy of that data. You can export your data and request its deletion at any time.
terms.ipBody: The Service, including its code, design, logo and content (excluding Customer Data), is owned by Smengo and protected by intellectual property law. We grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable right to use the Service for the duration of your subscription, in accordance with these Terms.
terms.disclaimerBody: We aim to keep the Service available around the clock, but provide it “as is” and “as available”, without warranties of uninterrupted or error-free operation. We may temporarily suspend the Service for maintenance or updates and will try to minimise such interruptions.
terms.liabilityBody: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Smengo is not liable for indirect or consequential damages, loss of profit, data or business arising from the use of, or inability to use, the Service. Our total aggregate liability under these Terms is limited to the amount you paid for the Service in the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to the claim. Nothing in these Terms limits liability that cannot be limited under applicable law.
terms.terminationBody: You may stop using the Service and delete your account at any time. We may suspend or terminate access for material breach of these Terms, with notice where reasonably possible. After termination or trial expiry your data is retained in read-only mode for a reasonable period so that you can export it, after which it may be deleted.
terms.changesBody: We may update these Terms from time to time. For material changes we will notify you by email or in the app at least 14 days before they take effect. Continued use of the Service after the changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.
terms.lawBody: These Terms are governed by the laws of Ukraine, unless mandatory consumer protection rules of your country of residence provide otherwise. The parties will first attempt to resolve any dispute by negotiation; if that fails, disputes are resolved by the competent courts.

privacy.metaDescription: How Smengo collects, uses and protects personal data.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Smengo (“we”) collects, uses and protects personal data when you use smengo.com and the Smengo application (the “Service”). It applies to website visitors, account holders and members of organisations that use the Service. We process personal data in accordance with applicable data protection law, including the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Law of Ukraine “On Personal Data Protection”.
privacy.collectBody:
• Account data: name, email address, password (stored in hashed form), interface language.
• Organisation data: company name, billing email, time zone, plan and subscription status.
• Employee data added by your organisation: names, departments, positions, work schedules and statuses.
• Payment data: processed by our payment provider Paddle; we do not store full card numbers.
• Usage data: actions in the app, pages visited, device and browser type, IP address.
• Support communications: messages you send us by email.
privacy.useBody: We use data to: provide and maintain the Service; create and secure accounts; process subscription payments; respond to support requests; send service emails (email confirmation, password reset, important notifications about the account or changes to terms); analyse aggregated usage to improve the product; ensure security and prevent abuse. We do not sell personal data and do not use Customer Data for advertising.
privacy.rolesBody: For account data of registered users and website analytics we act as the data controller. For employee data that organisations add to the Service, the organisation is the controller and Smengo acts as a processor: we process such data only on the organisation’s instructions to provide the Service. If you are an employee whose data was added by your employer, please direct requests about that data to your employer in the first instance.
privacy.legalBasesBody: We rely on the following legal bases: performance of a contract (providing the Service); legitimate interests (security, preventing abuse, product improvement); consent (where required, e.g. for non-essential analytics); compliance with legal obligations (accounting and tax records).
privacy.transfersBody: Our providers may process data in the European Union and the United States. Where data is transferred outside your jurisdiction, we rely on appropriate safeguards such as the EU Standard Contractual Clauses or equivalent mechanisms.
privacy.retentionBody: We keep account and organisation data while the account is active. After the trial expires without payment, data is preserved in read-only mode. If an account is deleted, we delete or anonymise personal data within a reasonable period (up to 90 days), except where longer retention is required by law (e.g. billing records).
privacy.rightsBody: Depending on applicable law, you have the right to: access your personal data; rectify inaccurate data; erase data; restrict or object to processing; data portability; withdraw consent at any time; and lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. To exercise your rights, contact us at hello@smengo.com.
privacy.securityBody: We use technical and organisational measures to protect data: encryption in transit (TLS), encrypted storage, access controls and row-level isolation of each organisation’s data, the principle of least privilege, and monitoring. No method of transmission or storage is completely secure, but we work to keep risks to a minimum.
privacy.cookiesBody: We use strictly necessary cookies (authentication session, interface language and theme) and analytics cookies that help us understand how the product is used. You can restrict cookies in your browser settings; necessary cookies are required for the Service to function.
privacy.childrenBody: The Service is intended for business use and is not addressed to children under 16. We do not knowingly collect data from children.
privacy.changesBody: We may update this Policy. We will post the new version on this page and, for material changes, notify you by email or in the app.
